title: To sort $$N$$ records by quick sort, the worst-case time complex
content:
To sort $$N$$ records by quick sort, the worst-case time complexity is $$O(NlogN)$$.
~@[](1)
author: DS课程组
description: To sort $$N$$ records by quick sort, the worst-case time complexity is $$O(NlogN)$$.

title: During the sorting, processing every element which is not yet at
content: During the sorting, processing every element which is not yet at its final position is called a "run". To sort a list of integers using quick sort,  it may reduce the total number of recursions by processing the small partion first in each run. ~@[](2)
author: 朱建科
description: During the sorting, processing every element which is not yet at its final position is called a "run". To sort a list of integers using quick sort,  it may reduce the total number of recursions by processing the small partion first in each run.

title: If there are less than 20 inversions in an integer array, the Qu
content: If there are less than 20 inversions in an integer array, the Quick Sort will be the best method among Quick Sort, Heap Sort and Insertion Sort.  ~@[](2)
author: 何钦铭
description: If there are less than 20 inversions in an integer array, the Quick Sort will be the best method among Quick Sort, Heap Sort and Insertion Sort.

title: During the sorting, processing every element which is not yet at
content:
During the sorting, processing every element which is not yet at its final position is called a "run".  Which of the following cannot be the result after the second run of quicksort? @[](2)

A. 5, 2, 16, 12, 28, 60, 32, 72
B. 2, 16, 5, 28, 12, 60, 32, 72
C. 2, 12, 16, 5, 28, 32, 72, 60
D. 5, 2, 12, 28, 16, 32, 72, 60
author: 考研真题
description: During the sorting, processing every element which is not yet at its final position is called a "run".  Which of the following cannot be the result after the second run of quicksort?

title: Given input { 321, 156, 57, 46, 28, 7, 331, 33, 34, 63 }. Which
author: 朱建科
description: Given input { 321, 156, 57, 46, 28, 7, 331, 33, 34, 63 }. Which one of the following is the result after the 2nd run of the Least Signification Digit (LSD) radix sort?

title: When running internal sorting, if merge sort is chosen instead o
content:
When running internal sorting, if merge sort is chosen instead of insertion sort, the possible reason should be: @[](2)

1. The code of merge sort is shorter
2. Merge sort takes less space
3. Merge sort runs faster

A. 2 only
B. 3 only
C. 1 and 2
D. 1 and 3
author: 考研试卷
description:
When running internal sorting, if merge sort is chosen instead of insertion sort, the possible reason should be:

1. The code of merge sort is shorter
2. Merge sort takes less space
3. Merge sort runs faster

title: Among the following sorting methods, which ones will be slowed d
content:
Among the following sorting methods, which ones will be slowed down if we store the elements in a linked structure instead of a sequential structure? @[](2)

1. Insertion sort;  2. Selection Sort;  3. Bubble sort;  4. Shell sort;  5. Heap sort

A. 1 and 2 only
B. 2 and 3 only
C. 3 and 4 only
D. 4 and 5 only
author: 考研试卷
description:
Among the following sorting methods, which ones will be slowed down if we store the elements in a linked structure instead of a sequential structure?

1. Insertion sort;  2. Selection Sort;  3. Bubble sort;  4. Shell sort;  5. Heap sort

title: When selecting a sorting algorithm, which of the following facto
content:
When selecting a sorting algorithm, which of the following factors must be taken into consideration besides the time and space complexities?  @[](1)

- I、the size of input data
- II、the structure used to store the data
- III、the stability of the algorithm
- IV、the initial condition of the data

A. III only
B. I and II only
C. II, III and IV only
D. I, II, III and IV
author: 考研真题
description:
When selecting a sorting algorithm, which of the following factors must be taken into consideration besides the time and space complexities?

- I、the size of input data
- II、the structure used to store the data
- III、the stability of the algorithm
- IV、the initial condition of the data
